scenario.context: Station 3 of the daily run: assess data transmission and network media.
scenario.mission: You assess data flow, media choices, and factors for stable client communication.

station.profile: Copper, wireless, fiber, and basic data flow in the client network

customerRequest.subject: Station 03: secure data transmission and media selection
customerRequest.body: Please assess the basic data transmission and the appropriate media selection for several workplace areas. We need a short, traceable recommendation for daily operations.

questions.title: Foundation of stable data transmission
questions.prompt: Which statement best describes the basis of reliable data transmission in the LAN?

options.text: A clean physical connection and matching standards are the foundation
options.rationale: Without a stable base, higher-level services cannot work reliably.

options.text: Only the device name decides the transmission quality
options.rationale: The name alone has no impact on physical or logical transmission.

options.text: As long as a cable is plugged in, every transmission is automatically error free
options.rationale: Even with a cable, standards, defects, or misconfigurations can interfere.

feedback.correct: Correct: Transmission starts with a stable technical foundation.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: weigh physical connection and standard compliance more strongly.

questions.title: Classify media professionally
questions.prompt: Which statements about network media are correct? (Multiple selections)

options.text: Copper is often practical for typical office distances
Fiber is suitable for longer distances and low susceptibility to interference
options.rationale: It offers advantages in reach and electromagnetic robustness.

options.text: Wi-Fi is flexible, but more prone to interference than a clean cable run
options.rationale: That is a typical practical difference.

options.text: All media are equally stable under all conditions
options.rationale: Environment and technology strongly influence stability.

options.text: Media selection should follow use case and environment
options.rationale: Only then does the technical decision fit.

feedback.correct: Correct: You assess media by use case and interference risk.
feedback.wrong: Partly wrong: classify flexibility, range, and interference risk more precisely.

questions.title: Data path from client to service
questions.prompt: Arrange the flow of a typical data transmission.
items: Switch or network forwards the data
Server or service processes the request
Network interface hands data to the medium
correctOrder: Network interface hands data to the medium
Switch or network forwards the data
Server or service processes the request

feedback.correct: Correct: This describes the data flow in a traceable way.
feedback.wrong: Not optimal: think from the application via the interface to the service.

questions.title: Estimate transmission time
questions.prompt: An 800 MB log archive is ideally transferred over 100 Mbit/s. About how many seconds does that take theoretically?

feedback.wrong: Please convert to Mbit first and then divide by the data rate.

questions.title: Media recommendation for two locations
questions.prompt: Recommend in 4-6 sentences a medium for two offices on the same floor and for a more distant technical room.
questions.expectedOutline: Location, media selection, and a short technical justification for both cases.

feedback.correct: Correct: The recommendation is practical and technically sound.
feedback.wrong: Please link location, medium, and justification more clearly.
